articles.title: Karnataka students record near-perfect pass percentage in ICSE and ISC exams
articles.description: Bengaluru: Karnataka students registered a pass percentage of 99.77% in Class X and 99.96% for Class XII under the Council for the Indian School Certi.

articles.title: Govt lowers export duties on diesel, ATF; keeps excise duty on petrol unchanged
Meta threatens to shut down social networks in New Mexico over child safety court case
articles.description: Company asserts in court filing that the state’s proposed remedies would be too onerous to comply with Meta has threatened to block access to Instagram , Facebook and WhatsApp in New Mexico , which would be an unprecedented move in its home country. The ultimatum, made in a court filing this week, comes after the company was found liable and fined $375m for child safety failures in a landmark lawsuit brought by the state’s attorney general. The second phase of the suit, known as the remedies phase, is scheduled to begin on Monday and will determine what actions the tech giant is obligated to take in response. Should Meta lose the second phase of trial, which will begin on 4 May, it would be compelled to introduce a series of reforms to its products. The New Mexico department of justice argues these changes would make Meta’s social networks safer for underage users in the state. Meta has argued these reforms are unfeasible and it would be left with little option but to withdraw its services completely. Continue reading...

articles.title: US oil reserves dropping sharply? Global oil prices swing as war disrupts supply and demand
articles.description: US oil reserves dropping sharply? Global oil markets face volatility as Brent and WTI crude move after a four-year high. Strategic Petroleum Reserve levels fall while exports rise. The Iran conflict disrupts shipping and fuel costs rise across transport and goods. Natural gas futures climb while Waha Hub prices stay negative due to pipeline limits.

articles.title: Evicted Bengaluru hawkers: Where do we go, how will we earn a living?
articles.description: Bengaluru: The Karnataka govt's announcement on Thursday that street vendors will be evicted from near Bowring Hospital has rattled hundreds of trader.

articles.title: Petrol, diesel, domestic LPG prices remain unchanged; no change in ATF price for domestic airlines
articles.description: Airline fuel costs are holding steady even as oil firms take the hit from rising global prices to keep fares manageable for travelers. Meanwhile, the rates for petrol, diesel, and domestic LPG cylinders haven’t changed, providing a buffer for nearly 90% of users against the fluctuations of the international market.

articles.title: Ashwini Vaishnaw flags off Jammu-Srinagar Vande Bharat Express
articles.description: A new 20-coach Vande Bharat Express now connects Jammu and Srinagar directly, significantly cutting travel time to five hours. This direct rail link, a first for the two cities, will operate six days a week starting May 2.

articles.title: PNG camps to be held in Kota from May 1
articles.description: RSGL to hold doorstep camps in Kota May 1-8 for domestic piped natural gas connections, offering on-the-spot registration and incentives to boost clean fuel access.

articles.title: PDA allows multiple family members to buy flats under new housing norms
articles.description: Prayagraj PDA adopts Model Costing Guidelines, letting multiple family members buy separate flats, boosting affordability and sales of unsold housing units.

articles.description: Delhi High Court seeks Delhi govt reply on claims lakhs of students lack textbooks; notice on contempt plea, status report ordered, supply promised by May.

articles.title: Oreo Firecracker Pop Cookies: When Can You Get the New Limited-Edition Summer Flavor Cookies?
articles.description: Firecracker Pop Oreos hit store shelves nationwide on May 4 for a limited time. Supplies are not guaranteed to last, so fans of limited-edition flavors will want to grab them sooner rather than later.

articles.title: Embassy Reit bets on acquisitions for growth, says CEO Amit Shetty
articles.description: Embassy REIT eyes acquisitions, redevelopment, and GCC-led demand to drive growth, betting on strong leasing momentum and rental upside

articles.title: Centre Revises Diesel, ATF Export Duties From May 1. Check New Prices
articles.description:
The Centre has revised export duties on diesel and aviation turbine fuel (ATF) for the next fortnight beginning May 1, while keeping petrol export duty unchanged at nil, according to a government notification issued on Thursday.

The export duty on diesel has been fixed at Rs 23 per litre, comprising a special additional excise duty (SAED) of Rs 23 per litre with no road and infrastructure cess (RIC). ATF exports will attract a levy of Rs 33 per litre (SAED only), while petrol exports will continue to remain duty-free, according to the statement by PIB.

The revised rates will come into effect from May 1, 2026, as per the gazette notification issued by the Finance Ministry.

Export levies on petrol, diesel and ATF were introduced from March 27, 2026, to ensure domestic availability of petroleum products by discouraging exports amid the West Asia crisis, the government said.

The rates are reviewed every fortnight and are linked to average international prices of crude oil and refined products during the review period. The previous revision came into effect on April 11.

The government also clarified that there is no change in excise duty on petrol and diesel meant for domestic consumption.

articles.title: NTPC’s Pakri Barwadih surpass 100 MT coal production
articles.description: Celebrating a major success, NTPC's Pakri Barwadih coal mining initiative has crossed the extraordinary threshold of 100 million tonnes of coal since its start in January 2017. Achieving such growth—a doubling of production in less than three years—speaks volumes about the project’s operational excellence and expansion strategies, reinforced by cutting-edge infrastructure like the continent's longest closed coal conveyor system.

articles.title: Crypto card spending surges to $600 million monthly: How are crypto cards becoming one of 2026’s fastest-growing payment trends?
articles.description: Crypto card spending growth 2026: Crypto cards are experiencing a surge in popularity, with monthly transaction volumes reaching approximately $600 million. This growth is driven by seamless integration with existing payment networks like Visa and Mastercard, enabling everyday spending of digital assets.

articles.title: DEA notice awaited on easing FDI rules for foreign cos with up to 10% in China firms
articles.description: FDI rules for overseas companies with up to 10% Chinese shareholding are set to be notified soon, as the Department of Economic Affairs is finalising the required approval under FEMA, an official said. The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) had already issued a March Press Note easing norms for investments from land-border countries, allowing such firms to invest in India under the automatic route across sectors.

articles.title: ‘Indian workers are central to Middle East oil — yet, they’re unseen’
articles.description: Indian migrants have been crucial to the Middle Eastern oil industry from its beginnings. Professor Andrea Wright highlights their significant contributions, from skilled tradesmen to manual laborers. Despite earning significantly more than in India, these workers often face loneliness and challenging conditions. Historically, strikes and unions were present but later restricted. Their labor remains vital, though often invisible.

articles.title: Govt slashes sugar cane commission
articles.description: In a bold move to rejuvenate the struggling sugar industry, Bihar's government has drastically reduced the Regional Development Council's commission by 90%, lowering it from 1.80% to a mere 0.2% of the cane price. This strategic decision aims to improve the financial health of sugar mills while ensuring that farmers' interests remain protected.

articles.title: Karnataka Cabinet approves Integrated Satellite Township project in Bengaluru South district at a cost of ₹18,133 crore
articles.description: The Integrated Satellite Township project in Bengaluru South district that had kicked up a political storm will be implemented at a cost of ₹18,133 crore. The State government gave in-principle approval to the project as well as compensation package to farmers on Thursday.

articles.title: ‘Normalise career breaks’: Techie quits LinkedIn, takes 1-year off to travel, returns to join Meta
articles.description: Taking a year off work to travel and rest seemed risky, but it paid off for this techie. His comeback to a top tech giant has sparked debate online about burnout and career breaks.

articles.title: Pune to develop 9 km road to connect Mumbai-Satara highway to Pune-Solapur highways
articles.description: The PMC has now allocated Rs 34 crore to carry out surveys, prepare detailed land acquisition proposals, and on-site planning for the development of 9 km long and 60 meter wide.

articles.title: Equitas Small Finance Bank Q4 profit soars 5-fold on expansion, asset quality
articles.description: Equitas Small Finance Bank's net profit surged five times to Rs 213 crore in the fourth quarter. This growth was fueled by expanded business and better asset quality. Provisions also decreased significantly. The bank saw its net interest margin improve. Gross advances grew by 22 percent year-on-year. Non-performing assets reduced, and credit costs declined.

articles.title: Buddha Purnima 2026 Bank Holiday: Are banks open or closed on May 1? Check details
articles.description: Bank Holiday on Buddha Purnima 2026: Even though banks will remain closed on national or public holidays, digital banking services will be available to the public 24/7. Services such as UPI, net banking, and ATMs will continue without any disturbance.

articles.title: Huge PF boost: EPFO plans 7.5x hike in minimum pension under EPS-95
articles.description: EPFO is considering major reforms including a minimum pension hike under EPS-95 up to Rs 7,500, ATM-based PF withdrawals, and a new e-PRAPTI portal for inactive accounts.

articles.title: Meta loses 20 million users across WhatsApp, Facebook, Instagram and Messenger: Here’s what the company is blaming for this drop
articles.description: Meta reported a loss of 20 million daily active users across its apps, attributing the decline to geopolitical factors in Iran and Russia. Despite this user dip, the company is significantly increasing its AI investment to between $125 billion and $145 billion for 2026, while also undergoing workforce reductions and facing legal challenges regarding youth safety.

articles.title: DoT trims Vodafone Idea AGR dues to Rs 64,046 crore, allows staggered payments
articles.description: The Department of Telecommunications (DoT) has finalised the AGR dues of Vodafone Idea Limited at ₹64,046 crore as of December 31, 2025, lower than the earlier estimate of ₹87,695 crore, following directions from the Supreme Court of India.

articles.title: Supreme Court takes suo motu cognisance of delay in approval of resolution plans by NCLT
articles.description: A resolution plan is a statutory proposal under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) designed to revive a distressed company (corporate debtor) through restructuring or takeover

articles.title: Wipro is buying back shares at Rs 250. What your tax bill looks like depends on this factor
articles.description: Wipro's share buy-back offers Rs 250 per share, a premium over market price. New tax rules effective April 1, 2026, treat buy-back proceeds as capital gains. This makes tendering shares more tax-efficient for both promoters and non-promoters compared to selling on the open market.

articles.title: W.O.R.R is the worry. A four-way assault pummells Indian macro
articles.description: India faces a rare convergence of four macro shocks—war-driven oil surge, a record weak rupee, and a likely poor monsoon—raising inflation and growth risks. The Finance Ministry warns these interacting pressures could strain external balances and policy choices, even as strong domestic demand and financial resilience provide a partial buffer.

articles.title: OpenAI lawsuit: Elon Musk criticises Microsoft’s role and $10 billion backing
articles.description: Elon Musk questioned Microsoft’s $10 billion OpenAI investment in court, saying such funding implies expected returns while raising concerns about corporate influence over artificial general intelligence development.

articles.title: After Vedanta demerger, should you consider adding stock to your portfolio?
articles.description: Vedanta: The demerger will be executed as a simple vertical split -- one share of each company for every share of Vedanta held. The move will result in the creation and subsequent listing of four new entities on the stock exchanges.

articles.title: Two-wheelers, defence, Bajaj Finance: Narendra Solanki's top calls as Q4 results roll in
articles.description: Narendra Solanki of Anand Rathi Shares & Stock Brokers guides investors on current market trends. He highlights durable companies in autos, banks, defence, and NBFCs. Solanki suggests focusing on two-wheelers and commercial vehicles in autos. He advises caution with Bandhan Bank but favors ICICI Bank and Axis Bank. Defence stocks require a long-term view.

articles.title: Why Is Vedanta Stock Down By 65% Today? Know About Demerger, Price Discovery, Impact On Shareholders
articles.description: Shares of Vedanta trade at Rs 502.1 apiece on the NSE in the opening trade, which is 64.9 per cent lower than the previous close of Rs 773.6.

articles.title: Vedanta's 65% share price crash an illusion, the stock is down just 5%. Here's why
articles.description: Vedanta shares adjusted significantly post-demerger, appearing to crash but actually declining 5% as four entities (Aluminium, Power, Oil & Gas, Steel) were separated. This restructuring aims to unlock shareholder value by allowing independent businesses to be valued more fairly. The demerger, approved by NCLT, sees the base metals business remain with a restructured Vedanta, while new entities will list separately.
